1: The most directly Christologically loaded verse in all of Isaiah — quoted by Jesus in the Nazareth synagogue (Luke 4:18-19) as the text of his first public sermon. Rûaḥ ʾădōnāy YHWH ʿālay ("The Spirit of the Lord YHWH is upon me") — the double divine title (ʾădōnāy + YHWH) emphasizes the authority of the commissioning presence. Yaan māšaḥ ʾōtî (H4886, "because he has anointed me") — the anointing (māšaḥ) makes the speaker the māšîaḥ (Messiah/Anointed One); the anointing by the Spirit at baptism (Luke 3:22) is the moment Jesus receives what this verse describes. Lĕbassēr ʿănāwîm (H1319 + H6035, "to bring good news to the poor/humble") — bāśar (to bring good news) is the verb from which the Greek euangelion (gospel) derives; ʿănāwîm (the afflicted/poor in spirit) are those who cannot help themselves. Liqrōʾ šĕbûyîm dĕrôr (H7628 + H1865, "to proclaim liberty to the captives") — dĕrôr is the specific Jubilee-liberty word from Leviticus 25:10; the Jubilee year released slaves, canceled debts, and returned land. The MKT "to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives" keeps the wound-binding and the Jubilee-release as distinct acts.
2: Liqrōʾ šĕnat-rātsôn YHWH (H7121 + H8141 + H7522, "to proclaim the year of the LORD's favor") — the šĕnat-rātsôn (year of acceptance/pleasure) is the Jubilee year (Lev 25:10-17): debts canceled, slaves freed, land returned. Jesus quotes this phrase from the LXX (Luke 4:19: eniauton kyriou dekton) and stops reading precisely here — before the next phrase. Wĕyôm nāqām lēʾlōhênû ("and the day of vengeance of our God") — Jesus stops mid-sentence. Luke 4:20-21: "He rolled up the scroll... 'Today this scripture is fulfilled in your hearing.'" The stopping point is hermeneutically deliberate: the year of favor is now; the day of vengeance is deferred to the second coming (2 Thess 1:7-8). The MKT includes the full verse and notes in commentary that the Lukan citation stops at this point — the original text is not abridged, but the Servant applies only the first clause to his present ministry.
3: The triple exchange: lāśûm lăʾăbēlê Tsiyyôn ("to bestow on the mourners of Zion") — three pairs of opposites structuring the transformation: (1) pĕʾēr taḥat ʾēper (H6287 + H665, "beauty/headdress instead of ashes") — the word pĕʾēr (beautiful ornament) plays on ʾēper (ashes) through near-rhyme; (2) šemen śāśôn taḥat ʾēbel (H8057 + H60, "oil of gladness instead of mourning") — the anointing oil that signals celebration replaces the dust-and-sackcloth of grief; (3) maʿăṭeh tĕhillāh taḥat rûaḥ kēhāh (H4594 + H8416 + H3544, "mantle of praise instead of a spirit of faintness/despair") — the rûaḥ kēhāh (literally "dim spirit" or spirit of fainting) is replaced by a garment of praise. Wĕqōrāʾ lāhem ʾêlê hatstsedeq (H424 + H6664, "called oaks of righteousness") — the people become tree-like in their rootedness and uprightness. 2 Corinthians 5:17: "Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!" — the exchange of v3 is the structure of new-creation transformation. The MKT "beauty instead of ashes, the oil of gladness instead of mourning" keeps the word-play intact.
4: Ûbānû ḥorbôt ʿôlām (H1129 + H2723 + H5769, "they will rebuild the ancient ruins") — the restored community will not merely repair recent damage but ʿôlām-old ruins: the desolations that have accumulated through generations of covenant failure. Šomĕmôt riʾšōnîm yĕqômēmû ("they will raise up the former desolations") — šomēmāh (desolation) is the exile's technical term; the rebuilding reverses the desolation. Wĕḥiddĕšû ʿārê ḥorbāh ("they will renew the devastated cities") — the urban restoration implies a full social and institutional renewal. The pattern anticipates the new Jerusalem of Revelation 21:2 — not merely restored but newly descended from God, as the ultimate answer to every ancient ruin. The MKT "they will rebuild the ancient ruins and restore the places long devastated" renders the temporal depth of ʿôlām and riʾšōnîm.
5: Wĕʿāmĕdû zārîm wĕrāʿû tsōʾnkem ("foreigners will stand and feed your flocks") — the Gentile service to the covenant people inverts the exile: in Babylon, Israel served the empire; in restoration, the nations serve Israel. Ûbĕnê nēḵār ikkārêkem wĕkōrmêkem ("and the sons of foreigners will be your farmers and vine-dressers") — the agricultural labor of the covenant land done by the nations. This verse does not endorse exploitation but rather depicts a reversal of fortunes: the community that was enslaved will experience the covenant blessing of v7 (the double portion) while the nations participate in the covenant economy. Galatians 3:28-29 ("There is neither Jew nor Gentile... and you are Abraham's seed") is a different but related fulfillment: the nations are incorporated into covenant blessing, not merely serving Israel from outside.
6: Wĕʾattem kōhănê YHWH tiqkārēʾû (H3548, "you will be called priests of the LORD") — the priestly identity promised to all Israel at Sinai (Exod 19:6: "a kingdom of priests") is now actualized in the restoration. Mĕšārĕtê ʾĕlōhênû yēʾāmēr lākem (H8334, "ministers of our God will be what you are called") — both titles (kōhănê YHWH and mĕšārĕtê ʾĕlōhênû) are applied to the restored community. 1 Peter 2:9: "you are a chosen people, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, God's special possession" — applying this verse directly to the new covenant community. Revelation 1:6: "made us to be a kingdom and priests to serve his God and Father." The priestly nation of Isaiah 61:6 is the royal priesthood of 1 Peter 2 — the promise fulfilled in those who share in Christ's priestly office through union with him.
7: Taḥat bāštan miśneh yirĕšû (H1322 + H4932, "instead of your shame, a double portion") — the miśneh (double portion) is the firstborn son's inheritance (Deut 21:17); the restored community receives the eldest-son share after the shame of exile. Wĕḵilimmāh yārōnnû ḥelqām (H3639, "and instead of disgrace they will rejoice in their inheritance") — the humiliation of exile becomes the platform for double-rejoicing. Lāḵēn bĕʾartsām miśneh yirĕšû ("therefore in their land they will inherit a double portion") — the double inheritance includes the land. Śimḥat ʿôlām tihyeh lāhem (H8057 + H5769, "everlasting joy will be theirs") — the joy is not temporary but ʿôlām: the eschatological permanence that characterizes the new-creation blessings of the chapter.
8: Kî ʾănî YHWH ʾōhēb mišpāṭ (H157 + H4941, "for I, the LORD, love justice") — the self-grounding of the covenant promises in YHWH's own character: the restoration is not arbitrary but flows from his love of right order. Śōnēʾ gāzel bĕʿôlāh (H8130 + H1498, "I hate robbery in a burnt offering") — gāzel (robbery/extortion) in the context of worship: the idol nations' sacrificial systems involve stolen goods; or, YHWH hates when his own people bring stolen things as worship. Wĕnātattî pĕʿullātām beʾĕmet (H6468 + H571, "I will faithfully give them their reward") — divine recompense is grounded in ʾĕmet (truth/faithfulness), not merely power. Wĕbrît ʿôlām ʾekrôt lāhem (H1285 + H5769, "and I will make an everlasting covenant with them") — the same covenant-formula as 55:3 and Jeremiah 31:31-34: the new covenant established through Christ (Luke 22:20: "This cup is the new covenant in my blood").
9: Wĕnōdaʿ baggôyim zarʿām (H3045 + H1471 + H2233, "their offspring will be known among the nations") — the covenant community's restoration will be visible to the nations; the visibility that was shame in exile becomes recognition in restoration. Wĕtsetsāʾêhem bĕtôk hāʿammîm ("and their descendants among the peoples") — the covenant-seed spreads through the Gentile nations. Kol-rōʾêhem yakîrûm ("all who see them will acknowledge them") — the recognition is from the nations themselves; the restored community's blessing will be self-evident. Kî hēm zeraʿ bēraḵ YHWH (H1320 + H1288, "that they are the offspring the LORD has blessed") — the final identification: the visible blessing of the restored community is YHWH's blessing, not their own achievement. Galatians 3:29: "If you belong to Christ, then you are Abraham's seed, and heirs according to the promise" — the offspring blessed among the nations is the church among the Gentiles.
10: The communal response song: śôś ʾāśîś baYHWH (H7797, "I will greatly rejoice in the LORD") — the doubled verb (śôś ʾāśîś) is the emphatic infinitive absolute + finite verb construction expressing intensive rejoicing. Tāgēl napšî bēʾlōhāy (H1523 + H5315, "my soul will exult in my God") — the personal possession: bēʾlōhāy ("in my God") — covenant intimacy expressed in the first person. The bridal imagery: ḵa-ḥātān yĕkahēn pĕʾēr (H2860 + H6287, "as a bridegroom adorns himself like a priest") — the headdress (pĕʾēr) worn by the bridegroom is the same priestly ornament of v3 ("beauty instead of ashes"). Wĕḵa-kallāh tiʿdeh kēlêhā (H3618 + H5716, "and as a bride adorns herself with her jewels") — the bride's adornment. Revelation 19:7-9 ("Let us rejoice and be glad... for the wedding of the Lamb has come and his bride has made herself ready") and 21:2 ("I saw the Holy City, the new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride beautifully dressed for her husband") fulfill the bridal imagery of Isaiah 61:10. The MKT "as a bridegroom decks himself with a priestly crown" captures the priest-king-groom fusion of the image.
11: The organic-emergence simile: kî ḵāʾārets tôtsîʾ tsimḥāh (H3318 + H6779, "for as the earth makes vegetation spring forth") — the natural inevitability of germination applied to YHWH's purposes. Ûḵĕgannah zĕrûʿêhā tatsmiâḥ (H1588 + H2221, "and as a garden causes what is sown to spring up") — the garden (gān) recalls Eden and the garden of v3 (the restored Zion like Eden). Kēn ʾădōnāy YHWH yatsmîaḥ tsĕdāqāh (H6779 + H6666, "so the Lord YHWH will cause righteousness to spring up") — the tsĕdāqāh (righteousness/vindication) is as organic and inevitable as plant growth: it cannot be stopped once YHWH has planted it. Ûtĕhillāh nĕged kol-haggôyim ("and praise before all the nations") — the organic emergence of YHWH's righteousness will be visible to the nations as praise-generating. Romans 1:16-17: "I am not ashamed of the gospel... for in it the righteousness of God is revealed" — the tsĕdāqāh that springs up in Isaiah 61:11 is the gospel-righteousness revealed in Christ. The MKT "as a garden causes what is sown to spring up, so the Lord YHWH will cause righteousness and praise to spring up" keeps the double-springing structure.
